content:
Order of explanation
* The overall readability would benefit from reordering the sections for a more logical flow of mechanics: Actions => Movement => Captures => Prisoners.

* Reordering would also allow you to remove some redundancy, particularly the first paragraph in the current Movement section.

Actions
* Please format "x" and "y" in "(x, y)" using <var> tags. You may keep parentheses/comma unformatted or continue to wrap them in <const> if preferred.

Movements
* Please clarify whether movement is:
  - 4-directional (N, S, E, W), or
  - 8-directional (including diagonals).

* Please specify a deterministic rule (e.g. fixed direction priority order) when multiple shortest paths exist.

Capture
* Please specify whether anything happens when prey enters an enemy camp.

Game End
* "At least one player has all their elementals prisoner.": Needs a wording fix. Suggested rewrite:

At least one player has all of their elementals captured as prisoners.

Technical Details
* "The score of a team is the number of prisoners belonging to its prey team.": Sounds slightly awkward. Suggested rewrite:

The score of a team is the number of prisoners it has captured.

Inputs
* Please state in the following values are integers in the relevant descriptions:
  - mapSize
  - elementsCount
  - elementalId
  - x
  - y

Inputs / Default stub
* Please change "prisoner" from bool to int, as some language stubs don't provide a bool to the player (e.g. Lua, Pascal). Use 0 / 1 instead of false / true.

Output
* Please format "x" and "y" in "(x, y)" using <var> tags. You may keep parentheses/comma unformatted or continue to wrap them in <const> if preferred.

Constraints
* Consider clarifying camp distribution:
  - Does each player always have exactly one camp?
  - Is the number of camps and elementals guaranteed to be equal for all players?

Leagues
* Do you plan to introduce additional mechanics in later leagues? For example, the story mentions rescuing elementals; will that be implemented?

Referee / Viewer
* I'll perform some testing later.

Thanks.

Les règles me semblent claires.
Le jeu me plaît.
Mais il manque le boss dans l'arène pour passer à la ligue suivante.
Du coup je ne peux tester que le niveau de base.

Hi everyone,

I've just published a Work In Progress version of my multiplayer game Elemental Wars.
It's a 3-player tactical game based on a rock-paper-scissors dynamic (Water / Fire / Plant), with simultaneous captures and simple pathfinding-based movement.

At this stage, the core mechanics are in place:
- movement toward a target (1 step per turn),
- simultaneous captures,
- prisoners system,
- (later leagues) release mechanics.

I'm mainly looking for feedback on:
- clarity of the rules / statement,
- gameplay feel and balance,
- edge cases or unclear situations,
- overall fun factor

Don't hesitate to try it out and share your thoughts even early impressions are very helpful!
Thanks

Merci pour ton retour !

Tu as raison il n'y a pas encore de boss dans l'arène car nous ne sommes que 2 joueurs pour l'instant...
Il devrait apparaître dès qu'on sera un peu plus nombreux.

En attendant, tu peux tester les boss en changeant de ligue en haut à droite.
Ils sont bien présents et adaptés aux mécaniques de chaque niveau.

N'hésite pas si tu vois autre chose.

Thank you very much for the detailed review!

All the points have been addressed:
- sections reordered (Actions > Movement > Captures > Prisoners)
- redundant parts removed
- movement clarified (4 directions + deterministic rule)
- capture behavior clarified (including in camps)
- Game End and Technical Details reworded
- inputs standardized (integers, prisoner as 0/1)
- formatting fixed (<var> x, y)
- camp distribution clarified

Regarding leagues, there is a clear progression already implemented:
- League 1: 1 elemental per team (onboarding)
- League 2: multiple elementals per team
- League 3: prisoner release system

Thanks again for the review, I'll be happy to follow up after referee/viewer testing!
